_comment: XML / XXE payloads. ARBITER's API is JSON-only — if any route parses an XML body, it's a bug. expected_blocked=true everywhere: a route that processes these as XML (resolving external entities, expanding parameter entities, dereferencing DTDs) is exploitable. PASS = the route returns 4xx without resolving any entity. FAIL = stack trace, 500, or any sign the parser tried to fetch the external resource.

payloads.rationale: Canonical XXE — defines an external entity pointing at /etc/passwd and references it in the document body.

Billion-laughs (small variant — 3 levels of expansion only, to keep the payload corpus-friendly). An XML parser that expands entities will explode this into 125 'lol' copies; a hardened parser refuses or returns 4xx.
Parameter entity referencing an external DTD — the standard out-of-band exfil vector when the parser doesn't echo entity expansion in the response.
XXE-to-SSRF — entity points at the EC2 instance metadata service. A vulnerable parser would expose IMDSv1 IAM credentials in the response.
PHP base64 wrapper — useful when the file contains characters the XML parser would reject. Coverage of the cheat-sheet edge cases.
External DOCTYPE reference — variant that doesn't even need an internal entity declaration; the parser fetches and processes the remote DTD on its own.
